title: Form Response Viewer - Bigger view!
excerpt:
The Form Response Viewer presents a very small window of information relative to screen size. Please change this utilize the entire available screen for viewing responses.

posts.author: jcfretts (Jeremy Fretts)
posts.cookedHtml:
The Form Response Viewer presents a very small window of information relative to screen size. Please change this utilize the entire available screen for viewing responses.

We use forms for long-answer responses, and the current viewer makes viewing them very awkward, with much scrolling, and wastes a lot of screen real estate

posts.author: Anne165Hernadez (Anne Hernadez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello!
To make the Form Response Viewer utilize the entire screen, you can adjust its properties to maximize the window. Set the form to full screen by using this.WindowState = FormWindowState.Maximized; and this.FormBorderStyle = FormBorderStyle.None;. Additionally, ensure that inner panels stretch by setting their anchor properties with panel1.Anchor = AnchorStyles.Top | AnchorStyles.Right | AnchorStyles.Bottom | AnchorStyles.Left;. This will make the form and its inner panels expand to fill the entire screen, reducing the need for scrolling and making better use of the available screen space.

Best Regards,

posts.author: jcfretts (Jeremy Fretts)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks - This sounds like it should work - but where do I, as an end-user, make these changes?

posts.author: JenefaJ (Jenefa)
posts.cookedHtml:
You’re right — the current Form Response Viewer in monday can feel cramped, especially when working with long-answer responses. The small viewing window often forces unnecessary scrolling and doesn’t take advantage of the full screen.

With The PDF Maker’s Form Builder and Viewer, you can overcome this limitation:

  • Full-Screen Layouts → Responses can be displayed in a clear, formatted PDF that uses the entire page width instead of a narrow window.

  • Custom Design → Long-answer fields can be styled with spacing, section headers, and formatting so they’re easier to read.

  • Bulk Viewing → Instead of scrolling through one response at a time, you can generate multiple responses into PDFs and review them in a meeting-ready format.

  • Sharing & Storage → PDFs can be automatically attached back to board items or shared directly with colleagues, so you don’t need to rely on the built-in viewer at all.

This way, you can still collect long responses through forms in monday, but read and share them in a format that’s full-screen, clean, and much easier to digest.

Guide: How to use the forms feature in monday
